skill.name: browser-act
skill.description: Browser automation CLI for AI agents with stealth mode and captcha solving. Automates web navigation, form filling, data extraction, screenshots, and browser interaction via a structured command interface.
skill.summary: Browser automation CLI with stealth browsing and captcha solving for AI agents
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 283 potential issues, primarily false positives from markdown documentation of CLI commands. The skill uses a structured CLI interface for browser automation with transparent security documentation. All external command patterns are well-defined CLI invocations, not arbitrary shell execution. Network access is limited to documented cloud services for captcha solving. Filesystem access is scoped to browser profile directories. Human oversight mechanisms (policies, human-assist-url) provide appropriate safeguards.

content.user_title: Automate web browsers with stealth mode and captcha solving
content.value_statement: AI agents need reliable browser automation for web scraping, form filling, and data extraction. browser-act provides a structured CLI with anti-detection capabilities and built-in captcha solving.
actual_capabilities: Navigate websites and interact with page elements via index-based commands
Extract data using get markdown, get html, or get text commands
Take screenshots and record HAR network traces
Solve captchas automatically using the solve-captcha command
Manage multiple browser sessions with isolation via --session flag
Inspect network requests and filter by URL, type, or status code
limitations: Requires uv package manager and Python 3.12 to install
Cannot bypass paywalls or access behind-login content without provided credentials
Real Chrome mode requires user to have Chrome installed locally

use_cases.title: Extract product data from e-commerce sites
use_cases.description: Automate scraping product listings, prices, and reviews from websites with anti-scraping protection. Use stealth mode to avoid detection.

use_cases.title: Fill forms and submit data automatically
use_cases.description: Navigate multi-step forms, handle dropdowns and checkboxes, and submit data. Credential-based logins require human confirmation first.

use_cases.title: Monitor changing web content
use_cases.description: Take periodic screenshots or extract text to detect changes on monitored pages. Use parallel sessions to check multiple sites.

prompt_templates.title: Simple page extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the main content from {url} and return it as markdown
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick content retrieval without browser session management

prompt_templates.title: Form filling workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Open {url} in a new browser, find the login form, wait for it to load, and show me what fields are available

prompt_templates.title: Multi-step automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Navigate to {url}, click the element labeled '{element}', wait for the page to stabilize, then extract all headings

prompt_templates.title: Captcha handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Open {url}, attempt to solve any captcha present, then continue with the login flow

output_examples.output: Returns page content as markdown with titles, scores, and comments extracted
Returns indexed list: [1] input 'Search', [3] button 'Submit', [5] link 'About'

output_examples.input: browser-act navigate https://example.com && browser-act wait stable && browser-act screenshot
output_examples.output: Opens page, waits for stability, returns screenshot path

best_practices: Run wait stable and state after any action that changes the page content
Use stealth-extract for simple content retrieval without session overhead
Check references/policies.md at the start of every task for human assist triggers
anti_patterns: Do not fill credentials unless explicitly provided by the user
Do not chain commands that require parsing intermediate output
Do not assume element indices remain valid after page navigation

faq.question: What is the difference between Stealth and Real Chrome mode?
faq.answer: Stealth uses anti-detection fingerprinting for bot-protected sites. Real Chrome connects to your local Chrome with existing login sessions.

faq.question: How do I handle captchas?
faq.answer: Use browser-act solve-captcha when a captcha challenge appears on the page.

faq.question: Can I run multiple browser sessions in parallel?
faq.answer: Yes, use --session flag to create isolated sessions: browser-act --session task-a browser open <id> <url>

faq.question: How do I stop automation and get human help?
faq.answer: Policies trigger human assist automatically for credentials, failed captchas, payments, and stuck operations. Use human-assist-url to request remote access.
